summary: Iteration 2: Fixed CORS issue blocking frontend-backend integration. All 7 backend contract tests pass. Frontend pentest wizard flow works correctly including target selection, mode switching (Manual/AI Agent), pentest creation, and LiveTerminal WebSocket connection.
critical_code_review_comments: P0 useWebSocket.ts: Hook dependency stability is good - uses useRef for reconnectRef to avoid stale closures, heartbeat cleanup is proper with useEffect cleanup function
P0 useSessionBootstrap.ts: Uses cancelled flag pattern correctly to prevent state updates after unmount - session bootstrap executes once safely
P0 usePentestWizardController.ts: Controller extraction is clean, all callbacks use useCallback with proper dependencies
CORS fix applied: Changed allow_origins from '*' to specific origins ['http://localhost:3000', 'http://127.0.0.1:3000'] to work with credentials:include
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_UI_Target via UI for pentest wizard testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: CORS issue was fixed in server.py (line 14-20). All P0 hook stability items verified working. Pentest wizard flow tested end-to-end: login -> create target -> select target -> create pentest -> LiveTerminal connects. Backend contract tests all pass (7/7). Frontend components render correctly.
rca_of_the_issue: RCA: CORS policy error - backend had allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True which is invalid per CORS spec. When credentials mode is 'include', the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header cannot be a wildcard. Fix: Changed to explicit origins list.

mocked_apis_list: AI orchestrator deep execution logic is foundational/placeholder
Sandbox execution currently dispatch-summary placeholder, not full Docker runtime

frontend_ui_tests.pentest_wizard: PASS - target select, mode switch, create/start/stop buttons
